conflicts.description: Chapter 1 presents Martin as lying on his back when Nate enters, but chapter 2 and Mallick's later reconstruction treat Martin's original position as left side facing the window before Paul rolls him over for CPR.
conflicts.evidence: Chapter 1 back position at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.post_revision.md:501; chapter 2 original left-side position and roll to CPR at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.post_revision.md:528 and snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.post_revision.md:544; Mallick reconstruction at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.post_revision.md:2904.
conflicts.canonical_value: Martin was originally on his left side facing the window; Paul rolled him onto his back for CPR and then straightened the body.
conflicts.resolution_note: Chapter 2's CPR sequence and chapter 10's reconstruction both depend on the left-side starting position, so that version is more causally embedded than chapter 1's isolated back-position detail. The context pack also planned Paul's correction about the body's orientation, which supports treating chapter 1 as drift.

conflicts.description: Chapter 1 frames Nate's voicemail as a near-2 AM call just before he finds Martin, while chapter 6 recollection and chapter 10 phone evidence place the voicemail on Friday evening.
conflicts.evidence: Late-night call framing at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.post_revision.md:483; Friday-evening recollection at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.post_revision.md:1816; phone metadata at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.post_revision.md:2910.
conflicts.canonical_value: Nate left the voicemail at 8:47 PM Friday and deleted it around 3:15 AM Saturday.
conflicts.resolution_note: The Friday-evening version is repeated and concretely supported by device metadata, while the chapter 1 near-2 AM sequence would require rewriting later investigative logic. The sheet keeps the phone-evidence timeline and treats the chapter 1 timing as manuscript drift.

conflicts.description: Chapter 3 implies Tommy is using an upstairs guest room beside the shared bathroom, but chapter 17 says Martin always put Tommy in the first-floor green room off the hall.
conflicts.evidence: Upstairs movement and guest-room staging at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.post_revision.md:801, snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.post_revision.md:817, and snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.post_revision.md:819; green-room explanation at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.post_revision.md:6387, snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.post_revision.md:6391, and snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.post_revision.md:6393.
conflicts.canonical_value: Tommy's regular assigned room is the first-floor green room off the hall near the slider and kitchen.
conflicts.resolution_note: Chapter 17 ties the green room to Martin's long-term monitoring system, preserved Tommy-specific supplies, and Paul's later understanding of Martin's protection. That makes the green-room version more causally central than chapter 3's local upstairs-room drift.

conflicts.description: Chapter 14 discovery dialogue identifies Cliff's wound as being on the left temple, but chapter 15 forensic inspection places the split on the right temple.
conflicts.evidence: Discovery-scene left-temple references at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.post_revision.md:4706 and snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.post_revision.md:4726; forensic right-temple description at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.post_revision.md:4889.
conflicts.canonical_value: Cliff's head wound is on the right temple before he is held under.
conflicts.resolution_note: Mallick's close forensic pass is stronger evidence than Paul's dark dock-side glimpse during the shock of discovery, so the sheet adopts the right-temple wound as canon.

conflicts.description: The previous sheet collapsed Paul's will discovery and the RT-document revelation into chapter 11, but the manuscript separates them: chapter 11 gives Paul the inheritance fact, while chapter 16 gives him the RT contents and Tommy's murder motive.
conflicts.evidence: Paul learns Tommy gets the house at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.post_revision.md:3241 and deliberately leaves the RT envelope sealed at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.post_revision.md:3251 and snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.post_revision.md:3255; Paul reads the RT document with Joel at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.post_revision.md:5754 and links it to Tommy's motive at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.post_revision.md:5780 and snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.post_revision.md:5814.
conflicts.canonical_value: Paul learns in chapter 11 that Tommy inherits the house; he learns the RT document's contents and Tommy's likely motive in chapter 16.
conflicts.resolution_note: The sheet was updated because chapter 11 explicitly stops short of opening the RT envelope, while chapter 16 makes the document reading and motive inference a separate causal turn. This is a sheet correction to match the manuscript, not a case of repeated drift overriding canon.
